commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=43b28627f4987c49b3ea8bcccf75d36922f5bfb2)
commands.review_summary:
Changes the release workflow to dispatch Homebrew tap updates with a multi-arch artifact template and adds a `build-darwin` Make target for darwin/amd64 and darwin/arm64 binaries.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main dispatches only a darwin_arm64 Homebrew artifact while the public tap formula is arm64-pinned. The PR body proves local cross-compilation, but not the release/tap workflow path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer coordination across this release workflow and `steipete/homebrew-tap`; an automated repair in this repository alone cannot prove or fix the formula conversion.

target.title: Add support for intel macs
target.body:
The code builds (and works) for intel macs. However, there are no releases that actually get created (despite [the docs saying so](https://sonoscli.sh/install.html#prebuilt-release-archives)).

## Summary

Add Intel macOS support to the release/install path.

The GoReleaser config already included `darwin_amd64`, so the CLI itself can build for Intel macOS. The missing piece was downstream packaging: the release workflow was dispatching the Homebrew tap update with a hard-coded `darwin_arm64` artifact.

This updates the release workflow to pass the multi-arch artifact template so the tap updater can select both darwin_amd64 and darwin_arm64 artifacts.

Also adds a local make build-darwin target to build both macOS binaries explicitly.

## Verification

Verified local cross-compilation for both macOS targets:

GOOS=darwin GOARCH=amd64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/sonos
GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/sonos

Confirmed generated binaries report the expected Mach-O architectures: x86_64 and arm64.

```
 /tmp/sonoscli ❯ GOCACHE=/private/tmp/sonoscli/.gocache GOMODCACHE=/private/tmp/sonoscli/.gomodcache make build-darwin
mkdir -p bin
GOOS=darwin GOARCH=amd64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o bin/sonos-darwin-amd64 ./cmd/sonos
go: downloading github.com/spf13/cobra v1.10.2
go: downloading github.com/spf13/pflag v1.0.10
GOOS=darwin GOARCH=arm64 CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o bin/sonos-darwin-arm64 ./cmd/sonos

 /tmp/sonoscli ❯ l
total 104
drwxr-xr-x   4 streeter  wheel   128B May 31 08:09 bin/
-rw-r--r--   1 streeter  wheel    11K May 31 07:44 CHANGELOG.md
drwxr-xr-x   3 streeter  wheel    96B May 31 07:44 cmd/
-rw-r--r--   1 streeter  wheel   841B May 31 07:44 Dockerfile
drwxr-xr-x  15 streeter  wheel   480B May 31 07:44 docs/
-rw-r--r--   1 streeter  wheel   199B May 31 07:44 go.mod
-rw-r--r--   1 streeter  wheel   987B May 31 07:44 go.sum
drwxr-xr-x   8 streeter  wheel   256B May 31 07:44 internal/
-rw-r--r--   1 streeter  wheel   1.0K May 31 07:44 LICENSE
-rw-r--r--   1 streeter  wheel   1.2K May 31 07:47 Makefile
-rw-r--r--   1 streeter  wheel   477B May 31 07:44 package.json
-rw-r--r--   1 streeter  wheel    14K May 31 07:44 README.md
drwxr-xr-x   5 streeter  wheel   160B May 31 07:44 scripts/

 /tmp/sonoscli ❯ l bin
total 58408
-rwxr-xr-x  1 streeter  staff    15M May 31 08:09 sonos-darwin-amd64*
-rwxr-xr-x  1 streeter  staff    14M May 31 08:09 sonos-darwin-arm64*
```

## After this

Once intel builds are in the release pipeline, the next step will be to update the homebrew [formula](https://github.com/steipete/homebrew-tap) after this change lands. We cannot update the formula tap until this release lands, because the formula requires the build SHA from that release.
target.author: streeter
